definitions.text: To continue in (a course or mode of action); to not intermit or fall from; to uphold or maintain.
examples: to keep silence; to keep possession

definitions.text: To remain faithful to a given promise or word.
examples: to keep one's word; to keep one's promise

definitions.text: To hold the status of something. To maintain possession of.
examples: I keep a small stock of painkillers for emergencies.
I only meant to lend him the shirt at first, but it looked better on him than me, so I told him to keep it.

definitions.text: The main tower of a castle or fortress, located within the castle walls.
The food or money required to keep someone alive and healthy; one's support, maintenance.
examples: He works as a cobbler's apprentice for his keep.

definitions.text: The state of being kept; hence, the resulting condition; case.
